title: Nuclear Engineer — Neutron Transport Simulation
about: ASPL Fusion (Agnira Sanlayan Pvt. Ltd.) is an applied plasma and neutron-systems company working on accelerator-based BNCT, domestic medical-isotope production, and industrial neutron applications, within established Indian regulatory and safety frameworks. The simulation team owns the Monte Carlo neutron-transport pipeline from target geometry through to dose calculations, supporting both accelerator design and regulatory submissions.
description: Join the founding simulation team. Design and optimise the Beam Shaping Assembly using Monte Carlo neutron-transport codes, model isotope-production yields via Bateman-equation frameworks, and benchmark simulations against experimental neutron data from IPR beam-time campaigns.
responsibilities: Run MCNP6 and OpenMC simulations for Beam Shaping Assembly (BSA) optimisation
Model neutron spectra from the proton-lithium source
Calculate isotope-production yields using Bateman-equation framework
Benchmark simulated results against experimental data from IPR campaigns
Contribute to the Nuclear Knowledge Graph (NuKG) platform
Support patent filings with simulation evidence
Support regulatory submissions with simulation-backed safety analyses
requirements: M.Tech or PhD in Nuclear Engineering, Nuclear Physics, or equivalent
Hands-on proficiency in MCNP6 and/or OpenMC
Strong foundation in neutron transport, moderation, and shielding physics
Familiarity with nuclear cross-section databases (ENDF, JEFF, JENDL)
Programming skills in Python; C/C++ or Fortran helpful
4+ years of experience in radiation-transport simulation (academic or industry)
preferred: Experience with PHITS, FLUKA, or Geant4
Publications in neutron physics or medical physics
Experience with the AERB regulatory framework
Familiarity with CAD-to-geometry conversion tools (e.g. DAGMC)
benefits: Co-authorship on ASPL publications and patent filings (the default for technical roles)
Conference travel support (ANS, IEEE NPSS, IPAC, ASTRO, IAEA technical meetings and equivalent)
Direct mentorship from Prof. Prabhat Ranjan and Prof. Y.C. Saxena, plus weekly working-group technical syncs
Founding-team scope: shape your domain end-to-end as part of a small team scaling up
Core collaboration hours 10:00–18:00 IST with flexibility outside that window
